sector.name: Jewelry Retail
sector.description: Organised jewellery retailers scaling stores and brands

fyPerformance: FY26 total income crossed ₹75,000 Cr
Q4FY26 consolidated total income stood at ₹20,300 Cr (+46% YoY); Q4FY26 PAT stood at ₹1,179 Cr (+35% YoY)
Q4FY26 jewellery portfolio stood at ₹18,195 Cr (+50% YoY), excluding bullion and digi-gold sales
India jewellery income stood at ₹17,114 Cr (+46% YoY); Tanishq, Mia and Zoya income stood at ₹16,047 Cr (+48% YoY)
CaratLane income stood at ₹1,066 Cr (+22% YoY), with EBIT of ₹89 Cr and 8% EBIT margin

guidance.value: 10-12 stores across 2-3 cities before national rollout
May grow slightly below revenue due to mix impact

highlights: Management called Q4FY26 one of the strongest quarters in Titan's history
Gold and studded portfolios both grew 35% YoY in India, while buyer growth recovered to high single digits
Management said consumers have started accepting gold at higher levels
Titan completed 67% acquisition of Damas Jewellery during Q4FY26; Damas added 123 stores to the international network
Titan added 27 net jewellery stores in India during Q4FY26, including 8 Tanishq, 14 Mia and 5 CaratLane stores
Affordability levers include old gold exchange, 18-carat gold, 14-carat studded jewellery, grammage purchase plan and lightweight jewellery
Titan Company Ltd.quote: "Titan continues to outperform the sector on scale, brand strength, buyer growth recovery, store expansion, international optionality and category innovation."

fyPerformance: FY26 consolidated revenue stood at ₹35,740 Cr (+43% YoY); consolidated PBT stood at ₹1,802 Cr (+88% YoY)
FY26 consolidated PAT stood at ₹1,350 Cr (+89% YoY)
Q4FY26 consolidated revenue stood at ₹10,275 Cr (+66% YoY); Q4FY26 consolidated PAT stood at ₹410 Cr (+118% YoY)
Q4FY26 India revenue stood at ₹8,990 Cr vs ₹5,350 Cr last year; India PAT stood at ₹366 Cr (+97% YoY)
Candere Q4FY26 revenue stood at ₹131 Cr vs ₹28 Cr last year, and reported profit of ₹3 Cr vs loss of ₹12 Cr last year
highlights: Management called Q4FY26 a fantastic quarter and said momentum from Q3 continued strongly into Q4
Kalyan launched 129 showrooms across Kalyan and Candere formats in FY26
India non-GML debt reduced by around ₹360 Cr during the year and from ₹1,300 Cr to ₹300 Cr over the last 3 years
Candere grew revenue by 160% in FY26, supported by strong SSSG and aggressive network expansion
Candere turned PAT positive from H2FY26 and has a studded ratio of more than 70%
FOCO and hybrid store models continue to support faster expansion and better capital efficiency
Kalyan Jewellers India Ltd.quote: "Kalyan continues to outperform the sector on revenue growth, PAT growth, store expansion, Candere turnaround and debt reduction."

fyPerformance: FY26 consolidated revenue stood at ₹10,739 Cr (+40% YoY); gross profit stood at ₹1,302 Cr (+83% YoY)
FY26 EBITDA stood at ₹704 Cr (+90% YoY), with EBITDA margin of 6.6% (+180 bps YoY)
FY26 PAT stood at ₹410 Cr (+88% YoY), with PAT margin of 3.8% (+100 bps YoY)
Retail revenue stood at ₹8,131 Cr (+51% YoY); e-commerce revenue stood at ₹529 Cr (+105% YoY); franchise revenue stood at ₹1,292 Cr (+83% YoY)
Q4FY26 consolidated revenue stood at ₹3,544 Cr (+123% YoY); Q4FY26 SSSG stood at 86% YoY
highlights: FY26 was a defining year as PNG crossed ₹10,000 Cr revenue for the first time
Q4FY26 was supported by wedding demand, festive demand and event-led sales
Total store count stood at 78 stores across 36 cities after adding 25 stores during FY26
FY26 average revenue per store stood at ₹137.7 Cr; revenue per sq ft stood at ₹4.51 lakh
Q4FY26 margins were impacted by higher gold bars and coins mix, which rose to 40% of sales
Old gold exchange is currently around 40% of business and management expects it to move towards 50%+
P N Gadgil Jewellers Ltd.quote: "PNG is outperforming the sector on revenue growth, PAT growth, SSSG, store expansion and profitability improvement."

fyPerformance: FY26 revenue stood at ₹8,430 Cr (+33% YoY)
Q4FY26 revenue stood at ₹1,997 Cr (+45% YoY); Q4FY26 EBITDA stood at ₹274 Cr (+116% YoY)
Q4FY26 PAT stood at ₹157 Cr (+151% YoY)
FY26 top line CAGR over 5 years stood at 24%
Old gold exchange stood at 44% of FY26 revenue and around 50% of Q4FY26 revenue
Diamond volume grew 9% YoY and diamond value grew 32% YoY
highlights: FY26 was a record-breaking year for Senco from a revenue perspective
Growth was driven by higher gold prices, customer trust, new store openings and affordable design innovation
Senco is focusing on 9-carat and 14-carat jewellery in both gold and diamonds
Lower caratage jewellery, including 9-carat, 14-carat and 18-carat, forms around 13-15% of sales
Company created more than 1.5 lakh lightweight designs during the year
Expansion focus remains East India, Bengal, North India and Central India, with more franchise-led growth
Senco Gold Ltd.quote: "Senco is outperforming the sector on Q4 PAT growth, old gold exchange scale-up, diamond value growth and lower-caratage affordability products."

fyPerformance: FY26 revenue stood at ₹4,070 Cr (+23% YoY); FY26 EBITDA stood at ₹310 Cr (+77% YoY)
FY26 EBITDA margin stood at 7.6%, up 234 bps YoY
FY26 PAT stood at ₹212 Cr (+88% YoY), with PAT margin of 5.2%, up 180 bps YoY
Q4FY26 revenue stood at ₹1,339 Cr (+87% YoY); Q4FY26 EBITDA stood at ₹73 Cr (+72% YoY)
Q4FY26 PAT stood at ₹51 Cr (+101% YoY); Q4FY26 SSG stood around 20%
highlights: Management highlighted that the jewellery industry is moving towards organised, transparent and trusted players
FY26 growth was mainly value-led, while volume declined around 20% because of high gold prices
Store network stood at 12 showrooms with total retail store area around 53,650 sq ft
Average revenue per sq ft stood around ₹7.6 lakh and average revenue per store stood around ₹339 Cr
Wedding jewellery contributed around 60% of product mix, while festival and lightweight jewellery contributed around 25%
Company launched DP Swarn Plus, a structured gold accumulation scheme that creates forward-booked demand and customer lock-in
D.P. Abhushan Ltd.quote: "D P Abhushan is outperforming the sector on PAT growth, EBITDA margin expansion and regional execution, but volume decline and inventory-led cash flow remain key monitorables."
